description: E2E browser integration tests using Ferrum (Ruby + CDP). Tests verify full user flows: wallet creation, live WebSocket prices, bet placement, settlement, history. No npm, no chromedriver.

userStories.title: E2E test scaffolding and infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the test infrastructure set up so I can write E2E tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Create test/e2e/Gemfile with ferrum ~> 0.17 and minitest ~> 5.0 gems
Create test/e2e/test_helper.rb with Ferrum browser setup/teardown (use browser.quit not close)
test_helper.rb includes wait_for(selector, timeout) that polls DOM and raises on timeout
test_helper.rb includes wait_for_visible(selector, timeout) that checks element is not display:none
test_helper.rb includes wait_for_hidden(selector, timeout) that checks element is hidden or removed
test_helper.rb includes wait_for_text(selector, text, timeout) that polls for text content
test_helper.rb includes wait_for_text_match(selector, pattern, timeout) for regex matching
test_helper.rb includes wait_for_class(selector, css_class, timeout) for CSS class presence
Browser headless by default, HEADLESS=false env var to watch visually
Base URL defaults to http://localhost:8080, configurable via APP_URL env var
Screenshots saved to test/e2e/screenshots/ on test failure
Helper methods: create_wallet(nickname, balance), wait_for_ws_connection, wait_for_prices, place_bet(symbol, direction, amount)
create_wallet fills #wallet-nickname and #wallet-initial-balance, clicks submit, waits for #wallet-modal to hide
wait_for_ws_connection waits for #conn-status to have class conn-status--connected
place_bet selects symbol from #bet-symbol, clicks direction button, enters amount in #bet-amount, clicks #bet-submit-btn, confirms via #bet-confirm-btn
A smoke test: page loads, h1.header__title text is 'Crypto Bet Engine'
On Linux (CI), Ferrum browser options include 'no-sandbox': nil
userStories.notes: Large story but all pieces depend on each other — can't split. This is pure setup, no app code changes except application-e2e.yml.

userStories.title: Wallet creation E2E test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that wallet creation works end-to-end in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Test opens APP_URL in fresh browser (no localStorage)
Validation test: submit with nickname less than 2 chars — #wallet-form-error shows error or HTML5 validation prevents submit
Validation test: submit with balance '0' — #wallet-form-error shows error
userStories.notes: Each test uses fresh browser context. Balance format is $1000.00 (no comma separator).

userStories.title: Live price display E2E test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that WebSocket prices appear in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates wallet via create_wallet helper
Waits for #conn-status to have class conn-status--connected (timeout 15s)
userStories.notes: Binance feed may take a few seconds to start sending prices. Use 15s timeout.

userStories.title: Bet placement E2E test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the bet placement flow works in the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates wallet and waits for prices via helpers
userStories.notes: Large story but each step depends on previous — cannot split without duplicating full setup.

userStories.title: Bet settlement E2E test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify that bet settlement updates the UI correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates wallet, waits for prices, places bet via place_bet helper
Waits for .active-bet__countdown text to contain 'Settling' (timeout 25s — 10s bet duration with e2e profile + 15s buffer)
Waits for #active-bets text to contain 'No active bets' (timeout 20s — settlement polling at 3s intervals)
Verifies #wallet-balance text differs from initial_balance (balance changed after settlement)
userStories.notes: Depends on application-e2e.yml from US-E01 to shorten bet duration to 10s. Without it, test would take 90+ seconds.

userStories.title: Bet history E2E test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the bet history view works after settlement.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates wallet, places bet, waits for settlement (reuse helpers)
Clicks .sidebar__link[data-view='history'] to navigate to history view
Verifies .history-table tbody tr exists (at least 1 row)
Verifies row text contains bet symbol and direction
Clicks .history-tab[data-filter='WON'] and waits briefly for filter
userStories.notes: Settlement must complete before navigating to history. Total wait time ~30s for this test.

userStories.title: Deposit E2E test
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify the deposit flow works.
acceptanceCriteria: Test creates wallet with balance 1000 via create_wallet helper
Waits for #deposit-toggle-btn to appear (dynamically rendered in wallet card)
userStories.notes: #deposit-toggle-btn is rendered by renderWalletCard() — must wait for wallet card to fully load before clicking.

userStories.title: E2E run script for local testing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a script to run E2E tests locally against Docker.
acceptanceCriteria: Create docker-compose.e2e.yml override that sets SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=prod,e2e on app service
Create test/e2e/run.sh that starts docker-compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.e2e.yml up --build -d
run.sh waits for app health via curl to /actuator/health (up to 60 retries, 2s apart)
run.sh runs: cd test/e2e && bundle exec ruby -e 'Dir.glob("test_*.rb").each{|f| require "./#{f}"}'
run.sh traps EXIT to run docker compose down
run.sh exits with test exit code (0 = pass, non-zero = fail)
run.sh supports HEADLESS=false env var passthrough for visual debugging
userStories.notes: Uses docker-compose.e2e.yml override to set e2e Spring profile without modifying the main docker-compose.yml.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions CI integration for E2E tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need E2E tests running automatically in GitHub Actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add e2e-tests job to .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs after existing test/build job
Installs google-chrome-stable via apt (NOT chromium-browser snap)
Installs Ruby 3.2+ via ruby/setup-ruby@v1 with bundler-cache: true
Uploads test/e2e/screenshots/ as artifact on failure (if: always(), actions/upload-artifact@v4)
Ferrum browser options include 'no-sandbox': nil on Linux (already handled in test_helper.rb)
userStories.notes: Depends on US-E08 run script. Cannot test CI locally — verify by reading the YAML carefully and checking against GitHub Actions docs.
